phase3_findings.description: Step 4 decline-surface lifecycle deadlock (step4-offer / step4-approval-after-exhausted-offer never recorded)
override-ground eligibility token renders token=none instead of stale-token (fail-open detectability)
transition-token assert comments claim marker/override-kind coverage the assert cannot provide (documented_falsehood, 2 sites)
hash_bytes accepts empty git object id on rc 0; override ground compares empty-to-empty as a match (fail-open)
_validate omits shape checks for the per-round routing booleans (fail-open of exactly-one-per-round bound)
SKILL.md fallback misroutes caller-input-validation breadcrumbs to the state-owner-unavailable fallback
record-degraded/record-dispatch degraded-arm sketches omit the required --round
reinit_forced launderable by a legal echo re-init after --force

vacuous ordinal-chain negative test + untested read-boundary after_round<floor_round fail-open gate; rounds_run value untested (false reconciliation-table claim); override-ground token staling untested
no-verdict-does-not-shadow-older-FILE behavior untested either way (pinned current behavior)
missing-file adversarial matrix row not driven across query classes

fix_decisions.claim_text: a no-verdict round should invalidate an older clean FILE round on unchanged bytes
fix_decisions.evidence: The behavior is defensible: the clean-scan breaks on REVISE (a positive needs-changes signal) but falls through no-verdict (an inconclusive re-audit, not a revocation); the bytes were genuinely cleanly audited in the FILE round and no revision invalidated them. The AC's 'until a later round completes with a verdict' addresses the no-prior-clean case. Pinned the current behavior with a test (T4) in both directions rather than changing it.

fix_decisions.claim_text: a post-fetch attestation tool error renders attestation=none, indistinguishable from never-attempted
fix_decisions.evidence: Real but narrow post-hoc observability edge (creation already succeeded; fetch succeeded but the attestation tool call itself errored). Distinguishing attestation=errored from =none needs a new summary token (design surface); low-impact relative to the fix-loop's cost. Advisory for the maintainer.

fix_decisions.claim_text: the three dispatch-* retry tokens are hand-enumerated in four uncoupled sites with no binding assert
fix_decisions.evidence: Maintainability nit (coupled-mirror). Extracting a _DISPATCH_RETRY_ACTIONS constant touches four working sites; the module already discloses the mirror-drift class. Advisory, not fixed in-loop to avoid churn.

fix_decisions.claim_text: StateError under-discriminates absent-vs-corrupt, forcing cmd_init to inspect message text
fix_decisions.evidence: Low-severity design wrinkle; works via message inspection today. A subclass/attribute is polish. Advisory.

fix_decisions.claim_text: the summary-line prose does not mention reinit_forced
fix_decisions.evidence: Very minor doc-completeness; the tool emits reinit_forced regardless and the prose already lists the principal fields. Deferred.

skipped_checks.reason: working tree dirty at run time (grades committed HEAD); runs in CI on a clean checkout with fetch-depth 0. stale-prose-lint on the committed diff reports 0 STALE rows.

phase3_findings.description: iteration-1 F6 regression: state-file read/parse failure on a mutation unrouted; 'complete by construction' false
vacuous creation-attestation malformed row (sibling of iter-1 de-vacuumed ordinal row)

shadow.over_grade_calibration: SFH attestation=none (diagnostic-only, fails visibly) calibrated Important->Suggestion with recorded evidence; the two Important promote-drivers (F6 documented_falsehood, vacuous test) are non-demotable/observable — not over-graded
